Vue vs React: What's Actually Different, and How to Pick One (Plus Common Interview Questions)

What's actually different between Vue and React? This post starts from where each framework came from and what it was designed for, covers the framework-vs-library distinction, then compares ecosystem, learning curve and debugging to explain why React ended up more popular — handy for interviews too.
You Don't Have to Read Clean Code to Write Decent Code

This post took me close to a year to write — it's a collection of real code smells I've seen in my time as a TA, anonymized and cleaned up. Not a Clean Code book recap; more like "here are common patterns that hurt readability, and how to write them so future-you (or your reviewer) doesn't suffer." Naming, var vs let/const, ES6 destructuring, comments, formatting, ternaries, loops, and not mixing Composition API with Options API.
React, You'll Learn It Eventually — Why Not Now? - Setting Up React with Vite - (22)
React, You'll Learn It Eventually — Why Not Now?

This is where we kick off a whole new chapter: Vite. A lot of you have probably already used Vite on a project, and if you haven't, no worries — I'll walk you through why Vite is so nice to work with, and how to actually use it. We'll build a Vite-for-React project from scratch, poke at its folder structure, and wire up Tailwind CSS along the way.
React Day 15: useReducer — useState's Grown-Up Cousin
You're Gonna Learn React Eventually, Why Not Now?

Next up is something kind of special: useReducer. You can think of it as a beefed-up version of useState. When your state starts getting complicated, useReducer gives you a much cleaner way to manage it, instead of juggling a single variable like you do with useState. We'll walk through the syntax, build a counter, and even fake a mini-Redux with useReducer + useContext.
Installing CRA Throws "You are running create-react-app, which is behind the latest release" — Here's the Fix

I needed an older version of Create React App for some testing, but kept hitting the "you are running create-react-app, which is behind the latest release" error. This post is just a quick note on how I got around it.
